description_long: This is a classic claw hammer featuring a robust metallic head and a straight, rectangular handle. The head is composed of two distinct parts: a flat, circular striking face for driving nails and a curved, bifurcated claw for prying and removing nails. The handle appears to be made of a dark, possibly synthetic or composite material, providing a secure grip. The metallic head has a slightly worn, matte finish, indicating durability and resistance to corrosion. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible embellishments or branding. The hammer is well-suited for construction, carpentry, and general repair tasks in urban environments.
description: A classic claw hammer with a metallic head and a straight, dark handle, featuring a flat striking face and a curved claw for nail removal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the hammer head, with the striking surface and the split claw clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the curved claw and the thickness of the metallic head, as well as the straight handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open claw and the back of the hammer head, with the handle extending downward.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the rounded striking face and the curvature of the claw, with the handle extending horizontally.
attribute_handle_type: Straight rectangular handle, likely made of plastic or composite material, with a smooth, non-textured surface and no visible ergonomic enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Claw hammer head with a flat, circular striking face and a curved, split claw for nail removal, made of steel.
